one_time_description: Prior year restatement to remove Gitxaala Trust from consolidated financial statements; Trust operates as a separate entity with its own Board
economic_notes: Gitxaala Enterprises LP (partnership), North Coast Skeena CFE, North-Co-Corp Ferry Services Ltd (33.33% interest). Guarantor on RBC loan for Tyee Building Supplies LP to $2,502,000 and RBC loan for Coast Industrial Construction Partnership to maximum $13,500,000. BC First Nations Gaming Revenue Sharing LP revenue. LNG proponent revenue and Provincial Coastal and LNG Benefit Agreements active.
notes: Comparative 2022 figures restated to remove Gitxaala Trust (decrease in cash $22,188,177, decrease in accounts receivable $500,000, decrease in operating surplus $22,688,177). Investments reported under modified equity method include Gitxaala Enterprises LP ($56,090,886), North Coast Skeena CFE ($1,964,251), and North-Co-Corp Ferry Services Ltd ($70,956). No schedule of remuneration for Chief, Council or staff provided. Deferred revenue moved from $3,999,152 to $0 during the year (full drawdown). CMHC revenue increased dramatically from $28,206 to $4,030,210 due to new capital funding. Total government transfers calculated as ISC + CMHC + DFO + FNESC + FNHA + Province of BC = 8,383,090 + 4,030,210 + 231,749 + 1,020,527 + 2,396,260 + 1,674,983 = 17,736,819.
